title: The Immortal Jellyfish
author: Marine Biology
content: The Turritopsis dohrnii jellyfish is biologically immortal because it can transform its adult cells back into their earliest polyp stage, effectively restarting its life cycle indefinitely.
action: This process, known as transdifferentiation, happens in response to physical damage or starvation.

title: Blue Whale Hearts
author: Cardiovascular Physiology
content: A blue whale's heart is so massive that it is roughly the size of a bumper car, and its primary artery is wide enough for a human to swim through, though it would be a tight squeeze.
action: Their massive body size requires an incredibly powerful cardiovascular system.

title: Rain on Other Planets
author: Planetary Science
content: On Saturn and Jupiter, the intense atmospheric pressure can turn carbon into diamonds, effectively causing it to 'rain' diamonds deep within their interiors.
action: Carbon is compressed by extreme heat and crushing gravity.

title: The Longevity of Lobsters
author: Genetic Biology
content: Lobsters do not biologically age in the way humans do because they produce an enzyme called telomerase that constantly repairs their DNA, allowing them to potentially live for over a century.
action: They only die from exhaustion during molting, predation, or disease.

title: Cleopatra’s Timeline
author: Historical Chronology
content: Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than she did to the construction of the Great Pyramid of Giza.
action: The Giza pyramids were finished around 2560 BCE, while Cleopatra died in 30 BCE.
